data.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (prior-art / novel-architecture justification). The plan crosses a bright-line (a new test-architecture pattern: a hermetic bidirectional round-trip assertion through the production path), so the prior-art overlay applies. Findings below; clean items affirmed.

AFFIRMATIONS (already satisfy T1):
- The "What" already names the concrete prior art it builds on — "using story A's fixtures/fake through the production path" — so the dependency on story A's FakeAcliClient (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/_fakes.py) is grounded, not reinvented. The fake's docstring even names story F as a downstream consumer, confirming the seam is intended for reuse.
- No unverified third-party capability assertion is made (the plan relies on in-repo fixtures/fakes and pytest xfail, both already present in the tree), so checklist item (c) passes.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Reconcile the proposed "hermetic bidirectional round-trip assertion" with the existing `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_roundtrip.py`, which ALREADY round-trips description/status/labels/parent/comments/links hermetically through the real pure differs (no live Jira). State explicitly whether the new test extends/replaces that module or is a genuinely distinct artifact, so this isn't an unacknowledged repackaging (Rule-of-Three).
  - criteria: T1
  - location: plan "What" — "A hermetic bidirectional round-trip assertion … for comments, issue-links (both directions), and parent, runnable in CI without live Jira"; codebase: tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_roundtrip.py (module docstring lines 1-28; sections "4. parent reparent round-trip", "6. link round-trip", comment self-echo)
  - evidence: existing module docstring states "These tests chain the REAL pure differs end to end against mock fixtures (no live Jira)" and the assertion is "after the round-trip the inbound differ emits NO change"; it covers parent/labels/comments/links identity round-trips — substantially the same field set and the same hermetic-through-differs approach this AC1 proposes.

- [ ] Distinguish AC2/AC1 of this story from story B's already-landed `jira_contract/test_snapshot_contract.py`, whose "Semantic round-trip" half drives FakeAcliClient → fetcher.fetch_snapshot → the inbound differ and asserts comment/issuelinks/parent are READ through the production path. Clarify what bidirectional coverage this story adds beyond B's producer→consumer round-trip so the keystone test isn't duplicated.
  - criteria: T1
  - location: plan AC line "[ ] A hermetic test asserts bidirectional round-trip of comments, links, and parent through the production path"; codebase: tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/test_snapshot_contract.py:1-18, 141 and docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md
  - evidence: test_snapshot_contract.py docstring: "Semantic round-trip — the inbound differ, run on the produced snapshot, actually READS comment / issuelinks / parent (+ a scalar exemplar)"; "Out: … the snapshot round-trip (story B)" appears in the epic roster, so the boundary between F's hermetic round-trip and B's round-trip needs an explicit demarcation to avoid repackaging.
